name: The Arsenal
special: Huntington Militia drills & special events; first-Sunday openings not consistently listed
discrepancy: Open days vary — some listings show first Sundays 1–4pm, the militia's own programming is event-based/by appointment. Confirm via the Huntington Militia before visiting.
note: A restored powder house and home where the Huntington Militia drills in full Revolutionary kit; costumed muskets and colonial demonstrations.

name: Conklin Farmhouse
special: Staffed by docents Sundays + by appointment; closed holidays. $5 tour
discrepancy: Sources disagree on open days (the Historical Society's own site says Sundays; town and other listings say Thu–Sat or Fri & Sun). Call 631-427-7045 to confirm before visiting.
note: The 1750 home of David Conklin, a Huntington patriot jailed by the British for signing the town's defiant 'Association' against the Crown.

name: Heckscher Museum Of Art
note: Long Island's art museum, set in leafy Heckscher Park, mounting a 250th show of American art alongside its permanent collection.

name: Jo-Ann Raia Archives
special: Town of Huntington Archives; weekday business hours, call ahead
note: The Town of Huntington's archives — named for longtime Town Clerk Jo-Ann Raia — safeguard original records dating back to Huntington's 1653 founding, including Revolutionary-era documents.

name: Ketewamoke Chapter House
special: DAR chapter house — open by appointment / for events; call ahead
note: The 1837 home of the Ketewamoke Chapter of the Daughters of the American Revolution, the oldest DAR chapter on Long Island outside New York City.

name: Lefferts Tide Mill Preserve
special: Private guided boat tours by advance registration; ages 12+ only
note: A rare c.1793 tide mill — the last of its kind still on its original site with its original gears — reached only by a short boat ride across Lloyd Harbor.

name: Caumsett State Historic Park Preserve
special: Passport-listed participating NY State Park; stamp availability is tied to NY State Parks RevWar 250 programs. Check the NY Parks event page for newly posted dates.
note: The former Marshall Field III estate on Lloyd Neck, now a state historic park preserve with miles of paths and shoreline; listed as a participating passport location.

name: The Suydam Homestead
note: A 1730s Greenlawn farmhouse currently closed while the historical society recruits volunteers to staff it.

name: Walt Whitman Birthplace Museum
addr: 246 Old Walt Whitman Road Huntington Station
special: Revolutionary Voices tours on select dates
note: The 1819 farmhouse where poet Walt Whitman was born, with period rooms and select Revolutionary-history tour days.

name: Joseph Lloyd Manor
special: Preservation Long Island
note: A 1766 manor occupied by the British during the war and once home to Jupiter Hammon, the enslaved poet who became the first published African American writer.

name: Suffolk County Vanderbilt Museum
special: Last admission 4:30pm
note: A waterfront Gold Coast mansion with a planetarium and a marine museum — the Hall of Fishes and Reichert Planetarium make it a full afternoon for families.

name: Bethpage State Park
special: Passport-listed participating NY State Park; stamp availability is tied to NY State Parks RevWar 250 programs. Check the NY Parks event page for newly posted dates.
note: Best known for its historic golf courses and broad parkland, Bethpage is one of the NY State Parks participating locations for passport stamp programming.

name: Sunken Meadow State Park
addr: Rte 25A and Sunken Meadow Parkway, Kings Park
special: Published RevWar 250 event: Hike Through History on Oct 4. Park grounds are generally open sunrise to sunset.
note: A north shore state park near the Fort Slongo Revolutionary War story, with NY State Parks history programming tied to the passport.

name: Cold Spring Harbor Library
special: Closed Sundays in summer
note: The town library displays three detailed dioramas of Revolutionary-era Cold Spring Harbor, a quiet gem for history-minded kids.

name: Northport Historical
special: Museum closed until later summer 2026; stamps at event booths (e.g. Huntington Independence Day, Jul 18)
note: Northport's history museum is between exhibits this summer, so it's giving out stamps from booths at community events like the July Independence Day celebration.

name: Old Methodist Church
special: Preservation LI gallery in the former Methodist church; seasonal house tours through mid-Oct
note: Preservation Long Island's exhibition gallery, set in a former 1840s Methodist church, showcasing Long Island decorative arts and a 250th-anniversary furniture exhibition.

name: The Whaling Museum
note: A hands-on maritime museum with real whaleboats and harpoons that's reliably a hit with younger kids.

name: Nissequogue River State Park
special: Published RevWar 250 event: Unfinished Revolutions: First Nations on Oct 17. Park grounds are generally open sunrise to sunset.
note: A state park on the Nissequogue River with trails, bluffs, and 2026 programming on Indigenous communities and the unfinished ideals of the Revolution.

name: The Nathaniel Conklin House
note: An 1803 homestead of one of Babylon's founding families, preserved as a house museum by the village historical society.

name: Village Of Babylon
note: The village's history museum lives inside the old Babylon Library, a handsome building now on the National Register of Historic Places.

name: Village Of Head Of The Harbor
special: Village Hall business hours; call to confirm stamp availability
note: The village hall of Head of the Harbor, a community that preserves the colonial-era character of the Stony Brook and Nissequogue waterfront.

name: Caleb Smith State Park Museum
special: Closed Mondays except holidays; NY State Parks RevWar 250 Hike Through History event Aug 15, 10:30am-12:30pm
note: A 1751 Smith-family home, now a nature museum at the heart of a 543-acre state preserve where the Smithtown founders farmed and lived through the Revolutionary era.

name: The Epenetus Smith Tavern
special: Buildings open for special events/tours; grounds open most days
note: A 1680s tavern that hosted town meetings and travelers along the old country road, preserved by the Smithtown Historical Society.

name: Widow Blydenburgh Tavern
special: Brecknock Hall, Peconic Landing; open for tours/events — check schedule
note: An 1857 Greenport manor built by the grandson of a Revolutionary-era settler, set on grounds overlooking the Sound and open for seasonal heritage programs.

name: Raynham Hall Museum
special: Admission $15 / $10 student / free under 6
note: The Townsend family home and a genuine Culper Spy Ring site — Robert Townsend was the ring's key New York agent, and a British officer courting his sister unwittingly aided the spies.

name: Oyster Bay Historical Society
special: House tours by appointment during gift-shop renovation
note: The Earle-Wightman House, a c.1720 dwelling with rooms interpreted to 1740 and 1830 and an authentic 18th-century garden, tells the story of Revolutionary-era Oyster Bay.

name: Inc. Village Of Lindenhurst
special: Check Village of Lindenhurst Facebook for stamp dates
note: Lindenhurst's village hall coordinates the community's 250th programming; watch their Facebook page for the stamp-event schedule.

name: Old Village Hall Museum
note: Housed in Lindenhurst's original 1901 village hall, with an exhibit on Jesse Ketcham, a local soldier who fought at the Battle of Brooklyn and later escorted Washington across Long Island.

name: County Of Suffolk
special: Rolling 2026 calendar — see suffolk250.org event map
note: The county's official 250th committee runs commemorative stamp events at fairs, parades, and civic functions all year — check their event map for the next one near you.

name: The Lauder Museum
note: Amityville's local history museum, set in a former bank building and run entirely by volunteers who can open it by request for passport visitors.

name: Nissequogue Village Hall
special: Village Hall business hours; call to confirm stamp availability
note: The village hall of Nissequogue, named for the Native people of the Nissequogue River and home to some of Smithtown's oldest colonial landholdings.

name: Leo P. Ostebo
special: Private group tours (up to 10); housed in the 1928 Osgood school
note: A community heritage museum tracing Kings Park from the Revolutionary War through the Spanish-American War and the Grumman space era, with a 2026 Revolutionary exhibit.

name: Nassau County Historical Society
special: Membership society (est. 1915); programs & lectures — check site
note: Founded in 1915, the Nassau County Historical Society documents the history of the towns and villages that border Suffolk on western Long Island.

name: Connetquot River State Park Preserve
special: Published RevWar 250 events: He Dared To Sign: William Floyd (Aug 2), Spy Through Parks (Sep 4), Revolutionary Remedies (Nov 14). Park is otherwise Tue-Sun year-round; closed Mondays except holidays.
note: A 3,473-acre preserve around the former South Side Sportsmen’s Club, with trails, river habitat, and Revolutionary-era programming through NY State Parks.

name: Hempstead Lake State Park
special: Passport-listed participating NY State Park; stamp availability is tied to NY State Parks RevWar 250 programs. Check the NY Parks event page for newly posted dates.
note: A Nassau County state park with lakefront trails and an environmental education center; listed by NY State Parks as a participating passport location.

name: Historic Heines Homestead
special: Central Islip Civic Council site; call for access
note: An early homestead preserved by the Central Islip community, recalling the farming families who worked this stretch of central Long Island through the colonial and Revolutionary periods.

name: Sagtikos Manor
special: Tours June–Sept; check site for days
note: A grand manor that served as a British headquarters during the Revolution — and where President Washington stayed overnight on his 1790 Long Island tour.

name: Jones Beach State Park
special: Published RevWar 250 events: FourLeaf Air Show and Fireworks over July 4 weekend as part of America 250 programming.
note: The iconic oceanfront state park is hosting America 250 programming during the July Fourth weekend and is listed as a participating passport location.

name: Jones Beach Energy & Nature Center
special: Passport-listed participating NY State Park; stamp availability is tied to NY State Parks RevWar 250 programs. Check the NY Parks event page for newly posted dates.
note: The education center at Jones Beach explores coastal ecology, energy, and resilience and is separately listed in the passport’s state park participating locations.

name: North Shore Historical Museum
special: Call or check site for current exhibition hours
note: Set in Glen Cove's restored 1907 Justices Court Building, this museum interprets the history of Long Island's North Shore, including its Gold Coast era.

name: Country House Restaurant
special: STAMP IS COLLECTED AT Stony Brook Village Center (111 Main St), Mon–Fri 9am–5pm — not at this house. The house itself is by appointment / Culper Spy Day only.
note: A 1710 farmhouse turned restaurant that served as a stagecoach stop and, by local lore, a meeting spot for Revolutionary figures passing through.

name: The Long Island Museum
special: Smithsonian Affiliate; free admission Thursdays for Three Village residents
note: 250th Revolution exhibit, a video-game exhibit, and the carriage collection. Very kid-friendly.

name: The Stony Brook Village Center
special: THE stamp pickup for all WMHO sites; comes with the audio walking tour
note: The heart of historic Stony Brook and the single pickup point for all the Ward Melville Heritage sites — collect your stamp here while doing the free audio walking tour.

name: The Three Village Inn
special: Stamp at Village Center, 111 Main St
note: A historic inn and restaurant anchoring Stony Brook Village; your stamp is collected just up the street at the Village Center.

name: The Washington Spy Trail
special: Stamp at WMHO Village Center, 111 Main St
note: A marked driving route tracing the path Washington's couriers and the 1790 presidential tour took across the North Shore.

name: St. John’S Episcopal Church
special: First three Sundays of each month; visitors welcome around services
note: An ornate 1765-rooted Episcopal parish on the former Nicoll/Idle Hour estate grounds in Oakdale, tied to one of Long Island's prominent colonial landholding families.

name: Roslyn Landmark Society
special: Guided house tours and a monthly lecture series; check event calendar
note: The preservation society of Roslyn, whose 340-year-old village center is a nationally recognized showcase of early American architecture saved from 1960s demolition.

name: The Brewster House
special: STAMP IS COLLECTED AT Stony Brook Village Center (111 Main St), Mon–Fri 9am–5pm — not at this house. The house itself is by appointment / Culper Spy Day only.
note: Built in 1665, it's the oldest house in the Town of Brookhaven; spy Caleb Brewster was a frequent visitor to the cove just beyond its door.

name: The Davis Town Meeting House
special: Monthly tours — call 631.846.1224 for the next date
note: A c.1740 tavern and inn that was the birthplace of several Coram patriots, now lovingly maintained by a volunteer society offering monthly tours.

name: Meadow Croft Estate
note: The summer home of John Ellis Roosevelt, first cousin of President Theodore Roosevelt — a Colonial Revival estate restored to its 1910 appearance with barns and a carriage house.

name: Patriots Rock Historic Site
special: Three Village Community Trust events; open Culper Spy Day (Oct 3)
note: The boulder marking the 1777 Battle of Setauket, where Patriot raiders attacked British troops dug in around the village church.

name: Sherwood-Jayne Farm
special: Grounds open; house by event/appointment (Preservation LI)
note: A c.1730 farm preserved by Preservation Long Island, with original wallpaper and outbuildings that open for special Revolutionary-era programs.

name: The Smith/De Zafra House
special: Docent-led tour events (Three Village Community Trust)
note: A Setauket landmark that once served as Brookhaven's first town hall, opened for docent-led tours by the community trust.

name: The Thompson House
special: STAMP IS COLLECTED AT Stony Brook Village Center (111 Main St), Mon–Fri 9am–5pm — not at this house. The house itself is by appointment / Culper Spy Day only.
note: Home of Dr. Samuel Thompson, who quietly treated members of the Culper Spy Ring; one of the oldest saltbox houses on Long Island.

name: Three Village
special: Stamp via Tri-Spy tours or museum open days — see tvhs.org
note: The 'SPIES!' exhibit here tells the full Culper Spy Ring story that inspired the TV series Turn — Setauket was the ring's home base.

name: Tri-Spy Tours
special: Scheduled walk/bike/kayak tours — book at culper.com
note: Guided walking, biking, and kayak tours that retrace the Culper Spy Ring's actual routes around Setauket harbor, stamping your passport along the way.

name: Long Island Maritime Museum
note: A 14-acre waterfront museum of Long Island's seafaring and oystering heritage, with National Historic Landmark vessels and a working boat shop on the Great South Bay.

name: Bayles Boat Shop
special: Website confirms the stamp is ready
note: A working wooden-boat shop where volunteers built a full replica of Caleb Brewster's armed whaleboat — the kind the Culper spies used to slip messages across the Sound to Washington.

name: Drowned Meadow Cottage Museum
special: Guided tours seasonally; free with Culper Spy Day. See @dmc_museum
note: A c.1755 cottage that was home to the Roe family, with an exhibit on the privateers who raided British shipping off Long Island's coast.

name: Long Island Explorium
special: Summer hours (Wed–Sun); $5 admission. School-year hours differ — confirm ahead
note: A hands-on children's science museum (STEAM) right on Port Jefferson harbor — the kind of place that pairs well with a stamp stop for younger kids.

name: Mather House Museum
special: Donation requested
note: An 1840s captain's house packed with Port Jefferson shipbuilding history and the remarkable Spinney Clock Collection of roughly 250 timepieces.

name: Greater Patchogue Historical Museum
special: Greater Patchogue Historical Society; call or check site for hours
note: The Greater Patchogue Historical Society preserves the history of Patchogue and its neighboring South Shore communities along the Great South Bay.

name: Miller Place/Mt. Sinai
special: Tours rest of year by appointment; 1720 William Miller House marks its 300th in 2026
note: The 1720 William Miller House — barely altered in 300 years — anchors a society preserving Miller Place and Mount Sinai, communities linked since the 1600s.

name: Freeport Memorial Library
special: Freeport Historical Society events via the library; confirm stamp hours
note: Freeport's library and historical society chronicle this South Shore village's history from its colonial roots as part of the Hempstead settlement.

name: Suffolk County Police Department
special: Stamps at National Night Out events; venues via @scpdhq
note: The police department hands out stamps at its 250th-themed National Night Out, a free family event with first-responder demos held at locations across Suffolk.

name: Avery Homestead County Park
note: A county park around a homestead farmed by the same family for 250+ years; Humphrey Avery III served here in the 3rd Regiment of the New York Line during the Revolution.

name: 1721 Noah Hallock Homestead
special: $5 donation; group/school tours by appointment
note: Rocky Point's oldest house (1721) and the birthplace of six Hallock men who fought as patriots in the Revolution — two are buried in the family cemetery on the hill behind it.

name: Swezey-Avey House
special: Yaphank Historical Society; archives/exhibits by appointment, monthly programs
note: An 1850s house cared for by the Yaphank Historical Society, in a hamlet whose mills and homesteads served the surrounding farms from the colonial era onward.

name: Bellport-Brookhaven
special: Other times by email: director@bbhsmuseum.com
note: The society's Barn Museum traces the area from its Native American roots through the Revolution, with its 2026 theme focused on life just before 1776.

name: Tesla Science Center At Wardenclyffe
special: Guided tours by advance reservation only
note: Nikola Tesla's last surviving laboratory, the Stanford White–designed lab where he pursued wireless power; a National Register landmark being restored into a science center.

name: Pagan Fletcher House
special: Pagan-Fletcher Restoration, Village of Valley Stream; call for hours
note: The Pagan-Fletcher house is Valley Stream's oldest surviving home, a restored 19th-century dwelling on land farmed since the colonial era.

name: Wading River Historical Society
special: Wading River Historical Society & Museum; call/check site for open hours
note: The Wading River Historical Society preserves the heritage of this old North Shore farming and milling hamlet at the eastern edge of Brookhaven Town.

name: Rock Hall Museum
special: House tours by appointment (groups up to 8); grounds open daily till 4
note: A 1767 Georgian mansion built for plantation owner Josiah Martin — one of the finest pre-Revolutionary War homes on Long Island, now run by the Town of Hempstead.

name: Manor Of St. George
addr: Neighborhood Road and William Floyd Parkway, Shirley
special: Suffolk County historic site; the 1693 manor where the 1780 Battle of Fort St. George was fought
note: The manor at the center of Long Island's Revolution: in 1780 Benjamin Tallmadge's raiders stormed the British-held Fort St. George here, then burned the King's hay stores at Coram.

name: Wertheim National
note: The visitor center for Long Island's national wildlife refuges, on the Carmans River — trails, exhibits, and a launch point for the area's Revolutionary-era river history.

name: William Floyd Estate
hours: Grounds daily 8am–5pm; Old Mastic House tours Wed–Sun
special: Grounds open daily year-round; guided house tours Wed–Sun in season (NPS)
discrepancy: Tour days/hours vary by source (NPS lists Wed–Sun in season; an older county listing said Fri–Sun 10–4). Check nps.gov/fiis or call before visiting.
note: The ancestral home of William Floyd, the only Suffolk County signer of the Declaration of Independence; a 25-room house on 613 acres run by the National Park Service.

name: The Suffolk County Sheriff’S Office
special: Closed holidays
note: Sheriff's office exhibit of Revolutionary-era artifacts and local history, displayed in the lobby of Riverhead Town Hall, with more pop-up exhibits rolling out across the county.

name: Suffolk County Clerk’S Archives
note: Home to a 1785 deed signed by Culper Spy Ring members Benjamin Tallmadge and Caleb Brewster — a rare original document from Washington's Long Island spy network.

name: Suffolk County Historical Society
special: 2026 exhibit "For the Blessings of Liberty: Suffolk County & the American Revolution"; $5 adult
note: Founded in 1886, Suffolk County's flagship history museum holds a major 250th exhibition on the county's Revolutionary role — from the Declaration's signers to the Culper spies.

name: The Riverhead Free Library
special: Public library local-history room; confirm stamp desk hours
note: Riverhead's public library holds a local-history collection documenting the colonial and Revolutionary roots of the county seat at the head of the Peconic River.

name: Hallock State Park Preserve
special: Passport-listed participating NY State Park; stamp availability is tied to NY State Parks RevWar 250 programs. Check the NY Parks event page for newly posted dates.
note: A shorefront state park preserve on Long Island Sound, near communities whose Patriots pledged loyalty to the Continental Congress in 1775.

name: The Greater Westhampton
special: Tuthill House Museum, 101 Mill Rd; call for current open hours
note: The Greater Westhampton Historical Museum preserves the heritage of the Westhamptons, Quogue, and Remsenburg — communities rooted in 17th-century colonial settlement.

name: Pond House
special: Quogue Historical Society / 1822 schoolhouse; call to confirm hours
note: The Pond House and the 1822 one-room Quogue schoolhouse preserve the history of this small South Fork village settled in the late 1600s.

name: 1799 Jesse Tuthill House
special: Mattituck-Laurel Historical Society; July 4 250th reenactment + Sunday Salons
note: The 1799 Jesse Tuthill House anchors the Mattituck-Laurel Historical Society's grounds, where a 2026 Revolutionary reenactment marks the nation's 250th.

name: Conscience Point Historic Site
note: The 1640 landing spot of Southampton's first European settlers, now a quiet nature preserve on the bay rather than a building.

name: North End Burial Ground
special: Historic burial ground — outdoor site, Southampton Town Historic Division
note: A colonial-era burial ground holding the graves of Southampton's early settlers and Revolutionary-era residents, open as a quiet historic landscape.

name: Pelletreau Silver Shop
special: Operator lists summer weekends; some sources list Tue–Sat 11–4. Working silversmith shop
discrepancy: Hours differ by source (the museum's own site says 'weekends in summer'; a landmark listing says Tue–Sat 11am–4pm; an aggregator says Wed–Mon 11–6). Call 631-283-2494 to confirm.
note: A working silversmith shop dating to 1686 — among the oldest continuously operating trade shops in the Americas; Capt. Elias Pelletreau supplied silver to clients including William Floyd.

name: Rogers Mansion Museum Complex
special: Interior closed for restoration; grounds open dawn–dusk
note: The Southampton History Museum's main campus around the Rogers Mansion; the interior is under restoration but the historic grounds remain open.

name: Southampton African
special: Group & co-founder tours by appointment; office Mon–Sat 9:00am–5:00pm
note: Set in a former barbershop that anchored Southampton's historic Black community, this museum tells the story of African Americans on the East End from the colonial era onward.

name: Thomas Halsey House & Gardens
special: Saturdays only, June 6–August 29, 2026
note: Thomas Halsey House — a 1660s homestead, one of the oldest in New York State, restored to its late-1600s appearance with a colonial garden.

name: Water Mill Museum
special: 41 Old Mill Road; call to confirm seasonal hours
note: A working 1644 water-powered grist mill — one of the oldest operating mills in America — where colonists ground grain through the Revolutionary era and beyond.

name: The Old House
hours: Family History Day & seasonal events — check council
special: Cutchogue-New Suffolk Historical Council; Village Green buildings
note: The Old House on Cutchogue's Village Green is a 1649 dwelling — among the oldest English-framed houses in the country — surrounded by a 1740 farmhouse, schoolhouse, and barns.

name: The Shelter Island History Museum
special: Havens House & history center, 16 S Ferry Rd
note: The Shelter Island Historical Society cares for the 1743 Havens House, home to a Quaker family whose island sheltered Friends fleeing religious persecution in the colonial era.

name: The Nathaniel Rogers House
special: Bridgehampton Museum; Corwith House by appointment (631-537-1088)
note: The Greek Revival Nathaniel Rogers House anchors the Bridgehampton Museum, interpreting the agricultural and maritime life of a community settled in 1656.

name: Heritage House
special: 2026 exhibit: "The Revolution at Hand"; Rev-War walking tours of the Old Burying Ground
note: The Annie Cooper Boyd House (c.1760), a colonial home on Captain's Row telling Sag Harbor's story — including the 1777 Patriot raid known as the Battle of Sag Harbor.

name: Sag Harbor Whaling And
special: Open ~6 months/year; check site for added weekdays in peak season
note: A National Treasure in the 1845 Benjamin Huntting whaling-merchant mansion, holding New York's largest whaling collection — Sag Harbor was British-occupied for seven years during the Revolution.

name: Custom House
special: Preservation Long Island; check tour calendar
note: The restored 1789 home and office of Henry Packer Dering, the first U.S. Customs Master of the Port of Sag Harbor — a window into the new nation's early maritime commerce.

name: Old Whalers’ Church
special: Active congregation (First Presbyterian); visitors welcome around services
note: The 1844 Old Whalers' Church, an extraordinary Egyptian-Revival landmark built at the height of Sag Harbor's whaling wealth; its lost steeple once guided ships home.

name: The Southold Historical Museum’S
special: Horton Point Lighthouse & Nautical Museum; $5/$10 family incl. both sites
note: An 1857 lighthouse whose construction was authorized by President George Washington himself, now a nautical museum overlooking Long Island Sound.

name: Southold Historical Museum’S
special: Maple Lane Campus — a dozen buildings dating to the 1700s; group tours year-round
note: The Southold Historical Museum's Maple Lane campus preserves over a dozen structures from the 1750s onward in one of New York's oldest English towns (settled 1640).

name: Southold Indian Museum
note: Run by the Long Island Chapter of the NY State Archaeological Association, this museum holds one of the region's great collections of Corchaug and Long Island Native American artifacts spanning 10,000 years.

name: Brecknock Hall
special: Closed Tuesdays; closed Jan 1–Memorial Day. Pay-what-you-wish
note: A maritime museum in Greenport's old railroad freight house, telling the story of an East End port whose ships and shipyards served the region from colonial times onward.

name: East End Seaport Museum
special: Closed Tuesdays; closed Jan 1–Memorial Day. Pay-what-you-wish
note: A maritime museum in Greenport's old railroad freight house, telling the story of an East End port whose ships and shipyards served the region from colonial times onward.

name: Ireland House Museum
special: Stirling Historical Society, Greenport; seasonal open hours — check site
note: The Ireland House preserves the maritime and village history of Greenport's Stirling neighborhood, a port whose ships and wharves trace back to the 18th century.

name: Guild Hall
special: Gallery admission always free
note: One of America's first multidisciplinary cultural centers (1931), Guild Hall sits in the heart of historic East Hampton, a village whose English settlement dates to 1648.

name: Mulford Farm
special: East Hampton Historical Society — hours vary by week; check calendar
note: One of America's most intact English colonial farmsteads, a 1680 farmhouse interpreted to the year 1790 with costumed interpreters and an original English-plan barn.

name: Oysterponds Historical Society
special: 2026: Webb House Tavern Night marks the 250th; $10 adults
note: The Oysterponds Historical Society in Orient cares for a cluster of 18th- and 19th-century buildings, including the Webb House where Revolutionary-era travelers once lodged.

name: Camp Hero State Park
special: Passport-listed participating NY State Park; stamp availability is tied to NY State Parks RevWar 250 programs. Check the NY Parks event page for newly posted dates.
note: A former coastal defense site at Montauk Point, now a state park with bluffs, trails, and Cold War-era military structures; listed by NY State Parks as a participating passport location.

name: Third House Nature Center
special: Third House Nature Center, Montauk County Park; seasonal programs
note: A historic Montauk ranch 'house' and nature center at the far eastern tip of Long Island, where colonial-era cattle were once driven to graze on the open Montauk grasslands.

name: The Soldiers & Sailors
note: The Huntington Historical Society's archive and gallery in the Soldiers & Sailors Memorial Building, with rotating exhibits on the town's founding era.
